'''Zebitrope IV''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="SWR" /> located in the [[Zebitrope system/Legends|Zebitrope system]],<ref name="CSWE Zebitrope" /> a part of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA Zebitrope" /> It occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position in its [[Star system/Legends|star system]],<ref name="EotE" /> which was connected to the [[Cadma system/Legends|Cadma system]] via the [[Cadma Conduit]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]].<ref name="TEA Zebitrope" />

A world of rolling [[Savanna/Legends|savannas]] and [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]],<ref name="SWR" /> Zebitrope IV was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of a<ref name="CSWE Zebitrope" /> rare<ref name="Gamer5">{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}}</ref> [[Unidentified lizard (Zebitrope IV)|species]] of [[Lizard/Legends|lizard]]<ref name="CSWE Zebitrope" /> that could also be found on the planet [[Zebitrope VIII]].<ref name="EotE" /> The lizards had a symbiotic relationship with a type of [[Unidentified mold|mold]]<ref name="CSWE Zebitrope" /> that only grew on the [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles']] backs. The Zebitrope IV lizard species also had a mutually beneficial relationship with an unusual type of [[Unidentified spongy growth|spongy growth]] that nurtured the reptiles and offered them concealment.<ref name="LCFO" /> The [[Fungus/Legends|mold]] growing on the lizards was the only source of<ref name="CSWE Zebitrope" /> the valuable and illegal drug [[lesai]], which was exported to the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]].<ref name="Gamer5" />

==Behind the scenes==
Zebitrope IV was first mentioned in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', the [[1983]] second entry in [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels by [[L. Neil Smith]].<ref name="LCFO" /> The planet's first full appearance came in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which also provided Zebitrope IV's only visual depiction—an image also used to illustrate eighteen other worlds in the game.<ref name="SWR" />

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed the Zebitrope [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Zebitrope IV, in the [[Zebitrope sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' later placed Zebitrope IV in the "Dufilvan sector,"<ref name="SWR" /> a misspelling of the [[Dufilvian sector/Legends|Dufilvian sector]].<ref name="Mid Rim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-sectors-of-the-mid-rim|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Sectors of the Mid Rim}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode both the ''Voyages SF'' 13 and ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' placements by establishing that the Zebitrope system was situated in grid square T-8 as part of the Centrality.<ref name="TEA Zebitrope" />

Information from ''The Essential Atlas'' created an apparent contradiction<ref name="TEA Zebitrope" /> with the mention of Zebitrope IV in ''Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon''. The latter work describes a collection of spongy growths that could be found on Zebitrope IV as having originated on a planet "a quarter of a [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] away" from the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Oseon 6845]] of the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]],<ref name="LCFO" /> which ''The Essential Atlas'' places in the same sector as the Zebitrope system.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
